body_txt: Christmas is a time of many traditions, and no doubt the most famous one is the Christmas Tree. When the holidays come, the tree is prominently placed in many a house and richly decorated with lights, ribbons and whatnot. The classic Christmas Tree is usually an evergreen conifer such as a spruce, pine or fir— either a real one or an artificial tree designed to look like one. However, for one reason or another, characters may not always be able to get their hands on such a tree, or they don't feel like doing what everybody else does and want to set themselves apart. The solution: just use a different kind of tree. Or if there’s no kind of tree available, a different plant. Or, if a character is either truly desperate or in a creative mood, something that isn’t a kind of vegetation at all. As long as it can be decorated with the traditional Christmas tree decorations, it can serve the part right? Popular choices include a palm tree in a tropical setting or a cactus in a dessert setting . Naturally, this trope tends to show up mostly in Christmas Episodes and Christmas Specials . It can also show up in stories set in a world where Christmas itself does not exist, but something similar does. Not to be confused with Aluminum Christmas Trees , which is used to describe things that actually exist despite sounding fictional at first.
